data.title: Southwest Airlines rallies to 52-week high after double upgrade from JPMorgan
data.description:
Southwest Airlines Co. (LUV) jumped in early trading on Friday after JPMorgan lifted the airline stock to an Overweight rating in a double upgrade from its prior ratings stance of Underweight.

Notably, analyst Jamie Baker and his team believe the potential for 2026 $5 EPS guidance from Southwest to be "attractively probable." That EPS guide would smash the consensus estimate of $3.03 for the year and be likely to push shares higher, according to Baker.

Southwest Airlines (LUV) was highlighted as possessing the airline industry’s deepest track record of profitability, an investment-grade balance sheet, and a loyal customer base. After operational hiccups and procrastination to adapt pressured the share price and investors’ patience, Baker and his team now believe the core business is starting to stabilize and believe initiatives (bag fees and seat assignments) are progressing as planned, as evidenced by management reiterating incremental EBIT targets throughout 2025, despite the demand headwinds in the industry. 

JPMorgan assigned a price target of $60 to Southwest Airlines (LUV).

Shares of Southwest Airlines (LUV) were up 3.8% in premarket action to $44.55 to mark a new 52-week high.

data.title: Mainstreet Equity doubles dividen to CAD 0.08
data.description:
• Mainstreet Equity Corp. (MEQ:CA) declares CAD 0.08/share quarterly dividend, 100% increase from prior dividend of CAD 0.04.
  • Forward yield 0.13%
  • Payable Jan. 31; for shareholders of record Jan. 16; ex-div Jan. 16.
  • See MEQ:CA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Ucommune gets Nasdaq notice for non-compliance
data.description:
• Ucommune International (UK) received written notification from the staff, indicating that for the last 34 consecutive business days, the closing bid price for the company's share was below the minimum bid price of $1.00 per share.
  • The Nasdaq notification letter has no current effect on the listing or trading of the company's shares.
  • The company may be eligible for an additional 180-day compliance period.

data.title: Liquidia reports $148.3M in 2025 YUTREPIA sales
data.description:
• Liquidia (LQDA) on Friday announced preliminary, unaudited full-year 2025 net product sales of YUTREPIA™ (treprostinil) inhalation powder, estimating $90.1 million in Q4 and $148.3 million for the full year, while also providing updates on its commercial launch and clinical development pipeline.

    It received more than 2,800 unique patient prescriptions since launch in June 2025, and generated more than $30 million of positive cash flow during the fourth quarter 2025.

data.title: Elon Musk’s xAI restricts Grok images to paying users after deepfake outcry
data.description:
Elon Musk’s AI start up xAI (X.AI) has restricted the use of AI service Grok's image generation features to paying subscribers after a growing outcry over its use for sexualized deepfakes of women and children.

"Image generation and editing are currently limited to paying subscribers. You can subscribe to unlock these features," said a post on Grok's account on X.

"Legacy Media Lies," said xAI in an email to Seeking Alpha when requesting a statement regarding the limiting of image generation and editing on Grok.

On Jan. 3, Musk posted on X that, "anyone using Grok to make illegal content will suffer the same consequences as if they upload illegal content." 

On Thursday, the European Commission ordered X to retain all documents relating to Grok for longer while the bloc ensures compliance with its rules after condemning it for producing sexualized images, Reuters reported. The Commission has decided to extend a retention order sent to X last year, which related to algorithms and dissemination of illegal content, prolonging it to the end of 2026.

Sweden also joined the chorus of criticism, saying the AI-generated images were unacceptable, after the country's deputy prime minister was targeted by a Grok user's prompt this week.

While xAI said it had taken down illegal AI-generated images of children, the latest incident could raise further concerns about how easy it is to override safety guardrails in AI models, the Financial Times reported.

data.title: Carter's says holiday quarter sales were up at a high single-digit pace
data.description:
Carter's (CRI) provided a guidance update on Friday ahead of its appearance at the ICR Conference.

On a preliminary basis, Carter's (CRI) expects sales to increase by a high single-digit percentage in Q4 over the prior year period. U.S. retail segment sales grew in the high single digits. U.S. retail comparable sales increased in the mid-single-digits, driven principally by strong eCommerce channel demand. Sales in the U.S. wholesale segment grew in the low single digits and the international segment achieved high single-digit growth.

"Fourth quarter comparable retail sales grew for the third consecutive quarter, reflecting strong consumer response to product and marketing initiatives, both in store and online. We also continue to grow average unit retail pricing as we price up, including less promotional activity, an important element of ongoing efforts to offset the impact of higher tariffs and improve profitability," highlighted CEO Douglas Palladini.

Shares of Carter's (CRI) are up 6.8% for the early part of 2026. Short interest on CRI stands at 10.9% of the total float.

data.title: ChipMOS Q4 revenue grows nearly 21% Y/Y
data.description:
• ChipMOS Technologies (IMOS) reported unaudited consolidated Q4 revenue of NT$6.52B ($207.9M), up 6.1% from the prior quarter and 20.8% from a year earlier.
  • December 2025 revenue totaled NT$2.20B ($70.2M), rising 2.9% from November and 23.8% year over year.
  • IMOS +3.8% premarket to $34.53
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Iran cuts off internet as unrest spreads; Khamenei slams protesters and Trump
data.description:
Iran's government has cut off the internet and international telephone calls after nationwide protests called by the country's exiled crown prince escalated Thursday night.

Reza Pahlavi, exiled son of the late shah, called on Iranians to take to the streets on Thursday and Friday.

The civil unrest in Iran began on Dec. 28, when thousands of people took to the streets to protest soaring inflation and the Iranian rial's (IRR:USD) sharp decline. Protesters are now also demanding regime change.

Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei accused protesters of trying "to make the president of another country happy," referring to U.S. President Donald Trump. He warned that Trump will be brought down and Tehran would not tolerate people acting as "mercenaries for foreigners."

Meanwhile, Iranian state media claimed that "terrorist agents" of the U.S. and Israel set fires and sparked violence in the protests.

According to the U.S.-based Human Rights Activists News Agency, the crackdown on protesters led to at least 42 deaths and over 2,277 people have been detained. 

Trump previously warned of U.S. intervention if Iran killed peaceful protesters. He reiterated the warning on Thursday, saying Iran has "been told very strongly… that if they do that, they're going to have to pay hell."

"The unrest potentially represents the most serious challenge in decades to the Ayatollah-led theocratic regime," Saxo analysts noted. "Any collapse of the current government would carry significant geopolitical and energy-market implications."

-- The Associated Press contributed to this report.

data.title: Sanuwave Health expects record annual revenue
data.description:
• Sanuwave Health (SNWV) expects revenues in the range of $13.3M to $13.4M (vs. $13.35M estimated) for the fourth quarter of 2025, implying an increase of 29% to 30% over Q4 2024.
  • This number is within the range of guidance given in the company’s Q3 2025 earnings release issued on November 7, 2025.
  • Full-year 2025 revenue is expected in the range of $44.3M-$44.4M (vs. $44.33M estimated), up 36% vs. full-year 2024. 2025 annual revenues were an all-time record for the company.
  • SNWV +1.96% premarket to $31.2
  • Source: Press Release
